parentQuestion: A patient recovering from a thyroid surgery suddenly develops a hoarse, breathy ...

scaffolds.q: The vocal cords must move together to produce normal voice.
scaffolds.explain: Voice production requires coordinated vocal cord adduction (coming together) and tension control. Breathe in this knowledge:the mechanics — air from the lungs passes through closed vocal cords, making them vibrate. Paralysis of one cord means incomplete closure → breathy, weak voice.

scaffolds.q: Nerves running near surgical sites are at risk of being injured during the procedure.
scaffolds.explain: Iatrogenic (surgeon-caused) nerve injury is a known complication of surgery in areas with dense neurovascular anatomy. the neck is a danger zone: carotid arteries, jugular veins, cranial nerves, sympathetic chain. One wrong move, and critical function is lost.
